nodes.label: Phase 4 — characterization: the capability map
nodes.description: Characterizes the Phase-3 adopted cell across 7 controlled axes (A1-A7) vs a genuinely-tuned backprop ceiling and Mono-Forward, run for coverage not triage, before any further optimization. Verdict: a substrate-native continual learner, not a static-accuracy competitor.

nodes.label: the capability map (7-axis gap-to-BP surface)
nodes.description: WIN: A6 continual (decisive, BWT -0.02->-0.18 vs online-BP -0.83->-0.99), A2 ambient-dim (crosses above BP by dim 500), A3 depth-composition (w2 slope>0 across whole difficulty band), A4 depth-is-cheap (backward flat vs BP linear, 1.3x->6.8x, depth-gated). TRAIL: A1 difficulty (capture 0.92->0.68), A5 class-count (synth gap +0.23, real digits +0.03). NEGATIVE: A7 eval-time noise (retention 0.75 vs BP 0.88).

hyperedges.description: The seven orthogonal axes (A1-A7 = P4.0-P4.6) jointly compose the capability map: WIN on continual (A6, decisive), ambient-dim (A2), depth-composition (A3), depth-is-cheap (A4); TRAIL on difficulty (A1) and class-count (A5); NEGATIVE on eval-time noise (A7) -- together stating the thesis 'a substrate-native continual learner, not a static-accuracy competitor.'
